body.title: Palace-ize Akien's design rules as filing-time checks
body.result: 5 check-shape palace nodes + index under theigors/rules/ticket_design_checks/ (applies_when/check_body/failure_message) + bidirectional narrative backlinks. Commit 07b1c6cf. Unblocks T-ticket-template-structured + T-review-loads-palace-rules.
body.description:
Audit Akien's implicit design rules and ensure they live in theigors/rules/* as CHECK-SHAPED entries that /review can load and verify tickets against positively. Rules to cover: no-sqlite (data persistence must name the Postgres table), OOP-first (if ticket adds shared state across multiple functions, propose a class), docs-in-code (ticket names where docstring lives), no-new-memory-schemas (memory tickets use existing clan.memories / memory_palace tables), test-plan-or-why-not (tickets declare tests or justify absence).

Some of these likely already have palace entries (e.g., theigors/rules/docs-live-in-code, theigors/rules/database) but in narrative form, not check shape. Audit first — inventory what exists and what is missing — then restructure to check-shape. New check entries should follow a consistent shape with: title, applies_when (predicate), check_body (what to look for in a ticket draft), failure_message (amend guidance).

Proposed palace path: theigors/rules/ticket_design_checks/<check-slug>; leave the existing narrative rules as the human-reading source, with pointers between the two.

Why: see feedback_scaffold_not_correct.md. Making rules visible at filing-time so the build naturally falls within them; scaffold not correction.

test_plan: after restructuring, verify that palace_tree returns N check-shaped entries under theigors/rules/ticket_design_checks; dry-run consumption from a /review prototype against one sample ticket.
